headline: Colorado's Blowout Signals West's Power Shift
subhead: Week 2 saw the playoff picture shuffle as traditional powers stumbled and upstarts flexed muscle.
lead: Colorado's 42-28 dismantling of Georgia announced that the Buffaloes are no longer a feel-good story—they're a legitimate threat in the upper echelon. Meanwhile, Tennessee held firm in the power poll's top spot despite a tight win over Penn State, but the real story belongs to the programs that validated the preseason hype with overwhelming performance.

segments.title: Colorado Arrives with Statement Win
segments.body: Colorado's 42-28 victory over Georgia wasn't a close call or a defensive grind; it was a statement that the Buffaloes belong in any conversation about the nation's elite. The offensive line controlled the line of scrimmage, the defense generated consistent pressure, and the coaching staff's game plan neutralized Georgia's strengths. This wasn't a fluke result—Colorado moved with the decisiveness of a team that knows exactly what it is.

segments.title: Texas Stuns Ohio State in Columbus
segments.body: Ohio State entered the week ranked fourth in the power poll; Texas left with a 29-15 win that exposed clear problems in the Buckeyes' defensive structure. Texas's defense suffocated the Ohio State offense early and often, setting a tone that the home team never quite recovered from. For a program with Ohio State's resources, this kind of home loss to a peer is the sort of result that reverberates through the locker room for weeks.

segments.title: LSU-Oregon Dogfight Delivers Drama
segments.body: LSU's 27-24 escape against Oregon featured explosive plays from both sides, with neither defense able to establish sustained dominance. The Tigers' ability to close out a game that Oregon had every chance to steal suggests a mental toughness that will be tested again in tighter moments. Oregon's effort was unmistakable, but the Ducks will need to convert these near-misses into actual wins to stay in contention.

segments.title: Notre Dame Shuts Door on Arkansas
segments.body: Arkansas managed just 10 points against Notre Dame's defense, which was suffocating from snap one. The Irish held the Razorbacks to ineffective drives throughout, never allowing rhythm or momentum to build. This kind of defensive dominance—35-10 final—suggests Notre Dame's front seven is elite enough to grind down most offenses week in and week out.

segments.title: Tennessee's Grip Tested in Tight Spot
segments.body: Tennessee's 26-20 survival against Penn State keeps the Volunteers atop the power poll, but the narrow margin reveals that this defense can be pressured into uncomfortable situations. Penn State made the Vols earn every yard in the second half, and a couple of defensive breakdowns away from a massive upset. Tennessee still looks dangerous, but the film shows cracks that future opponents will absolutely study.

closer: Week 3 will test whether Colorado's breakout is sustainable and whether Tennessee can silence doubters with a more convincing victory.
